description:
You are a network engineer who has just been asked to collect connectivity-troubleshooting logs on a machine whose clock appears to be drifting.  
    Before any packet-capture or traceroute information can be trusted, management wants every diagnostic file to be timestamped in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) using the unambiguous ISO-8601 “Zulu” notation.

    Your immediate goal is therefore the following:

    1. Create the directory  
       /home/user/net_diag  
       if it does not already exist.

    2. Generate ONE (1) single-line text file named  
       /home/user/net_diag/time_check.log

    3. That single line must contain ONLY the current UTC date-and-time in the exact ISO-8601 “basic” format:

           YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

       • Four-digit year (0000-9999)  
       • Two-digit month (01-12)  
       • Two-digit day of month (01-31)  
       • Capital letter “T” (literal)  
       • Two-digit hour, minute, second (00-23, 00-59, 00-59)  
       • Capital letter “Z” (literal, designating Zulu/UTC)

       Example of a *valid* line (DO NOT hard-code this value):  
           2023-08-22T14:07:03Z

       There must be **no** leading or trailing spaces, extra columns, or blank lines; exactly 20 characters followed by a single newline (ASCII 0x0A).

    4. Accomplish everything with a **single terminal command**.  
       (Hint: the standard GNU date utility can output UTC time directly without needing root privileges or altering the system-wide time-zone setting.)

    Automated grading will verify the following:
       • /home/user/net_diag exists and is a directory.  
       • /home/user/net_diag/time_check.log exists, is a regular file, and is world-readable.  
       • The file contains exactly one line, exactly 21 bytes long (20 visible characters + newline).  
       • The 20 visible characters match the regular expression:  
         ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$

    No other files should be modified or created.  Keep your terminal output concise; the essential artifact for grading is the time_check.log file described above.
truth:
Expected directory: /home/user/net_diag

    Expected file: /home/user/net_diag/time_check.log

    Verification details:
      • File must exist, permissions at least 644 (-rw-r--r--)  
      • stat --format=%s should return 21  
      • head -n1 should match regex ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$  
        (meaning exactly 20 visible characters)

    The autograder will NOT compare an exact timestamp (because the command is executed at runtime), but will insist on the precise pattern above and the newline length of 1.

    No other artefacts are checked.
name: task_000000_7892ecfb
